calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Continued To Sep-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for (petitioner) Adriana Nunez. Crisostomo Ibarra for and with (petitioner) Gloria Nunez.

The Court asked the parties where they are with mediation. Mr. Ibarra reported that mediation is set for August 15, 2016 with Betty Epstein and that Ms. Ebey has substituted in and they spoke on June 20, 2016. Ms. Ebey stated that both parties have filed a status on mediation. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary were continued to September 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are expected to advise the court as to the status of mediation by August 18, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Continued To Sep-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Bridges for and with Adriana Vanessa Nunez. Chrisostomo Ibarra for and with Gloria Nunez.

The Court stated that we have competing petitions, that Adriana is named in the will and asked if the parties have talked to each other. Mr. Bridges stated that Mr. Ibarra said he is not ready for mediation but that his client would like to mediate. Mr. Ibarra stated that he spoke with Mr. Bridges, that the difficulty with mediation is that Vanessa is harassing Gloria and her daughters and that it is difficult to envision mediation. The Court stated that that is what mediation is for, to get past the feelings and emotional responses, that it can rule if they do not want to mediate and confirmed that they qualify for Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Bridges reported that the notice to the minors was mailed directly to the minors. The Court stated that there are issues in Gloria's amended petition not properly addressed in the estate matter and that they need to be in a proper trust matter. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 20, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 21, 2016. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on July 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay The Unlawful Detainer Proceeding And Or Motion To Consolidate The Unlawful Detainer Proceeding With The Probate Proceeding; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For An Order Shortening Time Advance Hearing Date To Hear Gloria Guerrero Nunez Motion To Stay The Unlawful Detainer Proceeding And Or Motion To Consolidate The Unlawful Detainer Proceeding With The Probate Proceeding, To Be Heard By March 4, 2016; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Continued To Apr-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for and with Gloria Nunez. Mark Bridges for and with (respondent) Adriana Nunez. Court Interpreter Marta Riesen (spanish) for Gloria Nunez.

The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Marta Reisen. Her Certification Number is 300782. Her identification has been confirmed by a registered interpreter badge issued by the Judicial Council. The language to be interpreted is English to Spanish and Spanish to English. She has an oath on file with the Court.
The Court stated that we have a petition by Gloria Nunez, that there are objections by Adriana Nunez as to whether probate is needed and who has standing, that we do not have a competing petition, that Gloria's position is that the pre-nup is invalid, that it is not clear how that affects this petition, that Adriana has been designated as executor and the question is does Adriana plan to file a petition and what are her intentions. Mr. Bridges stated that he spoke with Mr. Ibarra briefly, that Mr. Ibarra stated that he had not known about the pre-nup, that the main asset is the house that was owned by the decedent prior to his marriage, that the trust leaves everything to his children, that there are no assets in the estate, that they had no intention of filing for probate as they did not think it necessary and that they believe the pre-nup is valid. The Court stated that it can be litigated as either a civil or probate action, that until this is done it will be an open question, that it does not know if there is potential community property and that in the meantime we have a pending petition for probate that may or may not have assets. Mr. Bridges stated that they will file a petition if the court thinks it needs to be done, that this is not a large estate and that they are trying to keep costs down. Mr. Ibarra stated that he spoke with Mr. Bridges, that he has looked at the pre-nup and that he believes there are community assets. The Court stated that the will names the executor and that there is nothing in your petition that suggest a basis for invalidity of the will. Mr. Ibarra stated that in August 2015 when the will and trust were executed the decedent was on morphine. The Court asked if he is now saying that the decedent lacked capacity. Mr. Ibarra stated that the evidence came out after the petition was filed. The court stated that it will continue the matter for Adriana to decide if she wants to file a competing petition and whether Gloria want to file an amended petition. The petition for letters of administration was continued to April 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
